title: Surgeries - Ellie Chowns MP for North Herefordshire
text_excerpt: Surgeries - Ellie Chowns MP for North Herefordshire Dr Ellie Chowns MP for North Herefordshire Work in Parliament Ellie’s Updates Ellie’s Plan About Ellie Surgeries Contact Sign up for Email Updates Latest News Dr Ellie Chowns MP for North Herefordshire Work in Parliament Ellie’s Updates Ellie’s Plan About Ellie Surgeries Contact Sign up for Email Updates Latest News Work in Parliament Ellie’s Updates Ellie’s Plan About Ellie Surgeries Contact Sign up for Email Updates Latest News Dr Ellie Chowns MP for North Herefordshire Work in Parliament Ellie’s Updates Ellie’s Plan About Ellie Surgeries Contact Sign up for Email Updates Latest News Surgeries Ellie holds regular surgeries at venues around the constituency. As you would expect from the use of the word surgery, it’s a chance to have a face to face conversation with Ellie about a matter that you wish to raise. Surgeries allow Ellie to gain a deeper understanding of how seriously an issue is impacting you personally or the community you live in, and explore ways in which the issue may be resolved or its impact reduced. When you first make contact, though email, phone or in person, Ellie’s constituency team initially undertake work on your issue. This route, through correspondence and conversations, may be more appropriate for you. This approach has the advantage of support in a more timely fashion, and is not dependent on surgery booking availability. A reminder that the ways to contact Ellie’s office are: Email : ellie.chowns.mp@parliament.uk Tel : 01568 630065 In person : the office at 9 The Buttercross, Leominster HR6 8BN is staffed Monday to Friday 9am to 5pm (aside from Bank Holidays; Christmas longer closure and occasional staff training days). If you do request a surgery booking, and you have been corresponding by email, you’ll usually be sent some links which enable you to make the booking for a location and time that is convenient to you. As mentioned above, we can make bookings over the phone or in person, but most people like to have the flexibility of finding a slot best suited to them through the links. The upcoming Surgery locations and dates are: 9th May in Bromyard in the morning 5th June in Weobley in the afternoon Ellie is committed to making her surgeries as accessible as possible. Here are some of the considerations: Booking process: Appointments can be made by phone, in person, by letter, or via email. Venue: Venues have been chosen to have step-free access, accessible toilets, and nearby parking, including accessible parking. Ideally they should be served by public transport. Venues should offer a calm, confidential environment with a staffed welcome desk to support visitors on arrival. Communication: The surgery appointment can also be offered online or by phone if attending in person is difficult. Additional support: Constituents are welcome to bring someone with them for support. We can also assist with communication needs, including large-print materials, arranging an interpreter, or other reasonable adjustments. At the start of the week of the surgery booking, you’ll be sent a reminder and the location of the surgery venue. Surgery bookings are 20 minutes long. Ellie will be accompanied by a caseworker who records the details that you share with Ellie while she listens. After a surgery, you’ll receive confirmation in writing of the agreed actions that Ellie will undertake, and some recommendations for you too, if appropriate. Some examples of matters that Ellie hears about in her constituency surgeries include: PIP delays Educational provision for autistic children Farmers’ concerns Support for families with disabled children.
